steps.title: Expand the binomials
steps.content:
Multiply inside the parentheses first. Distribute each term in the first factor across the second factor:
$$(x-\frac{5}{2})(x+12)=x^2+12x-\frac{5}{2}x-30$$
$$x^2+12x-\frac{5}{2}x-30=x^2+\frac{19}{2}x-30$$

steps.title: Distribute the outside factor
steps.content:
Now multiply the entire trinomial by $2$:
$$2(x^2+\frac{19}{2}x-30)=2x^2+19x-60$$
This matches choice C, $2x^2+19x-60$. The answer is C.
